name: Piedrabuena to Castillo Mirafores – circular from Tabla de la Yedra
name_original: Piedrabuena a Castillo Mirafores – circular desde Tabla de la Yedra
region_intro: Hiking around Piedrabuena offers a variety of routes exploring the region's natural and historical landscape. The area is characterized by its river systems, including the Bullaque and Guadiana, which provide scenic natural paths. Trails often feature gentle elevation changes, making them accessible for various fitness levels. Hikers can discover historical sites such as castles alongside tranquil natural settings. Best hiking trails around Piedrabuena The most popular hiking route is Río Bullaque – circular por Tabla de la Yedra, an easy 2.9 miles (4.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 13 minutes to complete. This circular path follows the Bullaque River, offering a tranquil experience. Another top favourite among local hikers is Camino Natural del Guadiana — circular desde Luciana, a moderate 5.6 miles (9.1 km) path. This route explores the natural path along the Guadiana River, starting from Luciana. Local hikers also love the Piedrabuena to Castillo Mirafores – circular from Tabla de la Yedra, an 8.9 miles (14.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain to a historic castle, often completed in about 3 hours 56 minutes. Hiking around Piedrabuena is defined by river valleys, natural paths, and historical landmarks like castles. The network offers options for both easy hikes Piedrabuena and more moderate routes. The routes in Piedrabuena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from 3 reviews. More than 40 hikers have used komoot to explore Piedrabuena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Piedrabuena?
A: Piedrabuena offers a diverse selection of over a dozen hiking trails. These routes cater to various fitness levels, with options ranging from easy riverside strolls to more challenging paths exploring historical sites.

Q: Are there easy hiking routes suitable for beginners in Piedrabuena?
A: Yes, Piedrabuena has several easy hiking routes perfect for beginners. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1094013256/rio-bullaque-circular-por-tabla-de-la-yedra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Bullaque – circular por Tabla de la Yedra&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 2.9-mile (4.7 km) circular trail that follows the tranquil Bullaque River.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see on hikes in Piedrabuena?
A: Hiking in Piedrabuena primarily features scenic river systems, including the Bullaque and Guadiana rivers, which carve out natural paths. You'll encounter varied terrain, from gentle riverbanks to areas with slight elevation changes, offering views of the local flora and fauna.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Piedrabuena?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Piedrabuena are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1198546491/camino-natural-del-guadiana-circular-desde-luciana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Natural del Guadiana — circular desde Luciana&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.6-mile (9.1 km) circular path along the Guadiana River.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I explore while hiking in Piedrabuena?
A: Piedrabuena's hiking trails often lead to or pass by historical landmarks. You can explore routes that take you to castles, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1094045016/piedrabuena-to-castillo-mirafores-circular-from-tabla-de-la-yedra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piedrabuena to Castillo Mirafores – circular from Tabla de la Yedra&lt;/a&gt; trail. Other notable points of interest in the area include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1064593" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sheep Bridge&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5896798" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vicario Dam&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Piedrabuena?
A: The trails in Piedrabuena are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.0 stars. Hikers frequently praise the tranquil river paths and the opportunity to discover historical sites like castles along the way.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of mountain peaks or elevated points?
A: While the region is known for its river valleys, some trails offer views of local elevated points. You can find highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2718219" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Picón&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6264366" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Almagrero&lt;/a&gt;, which provide higher vantage points for scenic views.

Q: Can I find trails that are suitable for families with children?
A: Yes, the area offers trails with gentle elevation changes and shorter distances, making them suitable for families. The easy circular routes along the rivers, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1094013256/rio-bullaque-circular-por-tabla-de-la-yedra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Bullaque – circular por Tabla de la Yedra&lt;/a&gt;, are often a good choice for family outings.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: The river systems and natural landscapes around Piedrabuena are home to various wildlife. You might spot diverse bird species, including storks, which have notable nesting sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3728650" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stork nests&lt;/a&gt; highlight. The tranquil environment also supports other local fauna.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by water features like reservoirs or beaches?
A: Yes, some routes in the Piedrabuena area are close to significant water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5896798" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vicario Dam&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5606327" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vicario Beaches&lt;/a&gt; are notable attractions near certain trails, offering opportunities to enjoy the water landscape.

Q: What is the typical duration for a moderate hike in Piedrabuena?
A: Moderate hikes in Piedrabuena can vary in length and duration. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1102647197/luciana-guadiana-river-el-chiquero-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Luciana - Guadiana River - El Chiquero Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 5.3 miles (8.6 km) long, typically takes around 2 hours 12 minutes to complete.

Q: Are there any trails that connect multiple historical castles?
A: Yes, for those interested in history, there are routes that allow you to visit multiple historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1197409802/mortara-castle-miraflores-castle-tabla-de-la-hiedra-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mortara Castle - Miraflores Castle - Tabla de la Hiedra Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.4-mile (10.3 km) route that connects two castles, offering a journey through the region's past.
tips: New Bridge in the Tabla de la Yedra - Piedrabuena, excellent hiking trails.

The Hermitage of the Santísimo Cristo de la Antigua, is a religious building that stands in the town of Piedrabuena. Inside, the image of the Santísimo Cristo de la Antigua, patron saint of the town, is venerated.

Located in the Maiyor square next to the Town Hall. It is a Neoclassical style construction. Among the construction materials, brick predominates, and of course volcanic stone, and the metallic roofs of the towers stand out. Very nice in general everything.

Miraflores Castle is a fortress of Islamic origin, dated from ceramic samples to around the 9th-10th centuries, although its chronology extends with various modifications until the 14th century. Built in tabiya and masonry, it is mainly located in the town of Piedrabuena.
The first documentary reference to the Miraflores Castle dates back to the 12th century. In a bull from Pope Gregory VIII of November 4, 1187, all the possessions of the Order of Calatrava are confirmed, among which are the castles of Caracuel, Alarcos, Benavente, Ciruela, Malagón, Guadalerza and Piedrabuena.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of the Most Holy Christ of the Ancient
